name: How much does a bathroom remodel typically cost in the Pacifica / Daly City area?
headline: How much does a bathroom remodel typically cost in the Pacifica / Daly City area?

mainEntity.name: How much does a bathroom remodel typically cost in the Pacifica / Daly City area?
mainEntity.text: How much does a bathroom remodel typically cost in the Pacifica / Daly City area?

acceptedAnswer.text: A realistic range for a full bathroom remodel in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and nearby coastal cities is usually **$20,000 to $60,000+**, depending on size and finishes. Labor in the Bay Area is higher than many parts of the country, and coastal homes may have extra costs from moisture damage, older plumbing, or code upgrades. A small guest bath with basic tile, stock vanity, and no layout changes might land in the $20,000–$30,000 range. A larger primary bath with custom tile, walk‑in shower, new lighting, and better ventilation can easily run $35,000–$60,000 or more. Moving plumbing, changing walls, or adding skylights will push the price higher. When budgeting, set aside at least 10–20% as a **contingency fund** for surprises inside walls, dry rot, or permitting delays. Ask for an itemized estimate so you can see where the money goes: demolition, plumbing, electrical, tile, fixtures, labor, and permits. This helps you decide where to save (for example, simpler tile patterns) and where to spend (like waterproofing and ventilation). A common pitfall is planning around the lowest quote without checking what’s actually included. Make sure each estimate covers permits, inspections, debris removal, and any code-required upgrades. If one price looks too good to be true, it often means something important is missing. Always get details in writing before work begins.
